description: Self-Benchmarking (H8): Implement anomaly-derived benchmark generation, blind spot discovery, diagnostic value metrics, and the self-benchmark cycle runner. This makes the eval system self-growing by turning failure signals into new evaluation pressure.
steps: 1. Create virtual_genesis/eval/benchmark_generator.py with generate_from_anomaly_candidates(anomalies) -> List[TaskCase]. Takes anomaly candidate dicts (as produced by extract_anomaly_candidates), creates diagnostic TaskCase objects targeting those anomaly patterns. Each generated task has diagnostic_purpose=['anomaly_derived', 'self_benchmark']. Use TaskCase.create() factory. Map anomaly source_type to appropriate task families and prompts.

2. Create virtual_genesis/eval/reports/blind_spot_discovery.py with discover_blind_spots(all_results, all_cases) -> dict. Analyzes which combinations of (family, perturbation_type, difficulty_class) have never been tested or always succeed (100% rate). Returns a BlindSpotReport dict with fields: untested_combinations (list), suspiciously_easy_regions (list of combos with 100% success), coverage_matrix (dict), coverage_ratio (float).

3. Create virtual_genesis/eval/reports/diagnostic_value.py with compute_diagnostic_value(case_results_across_conditions) -> float. For a given test case, measure how useful it is at discriminating between conditions. High value (near 1.0) = large spread in success/failure across conditions. Low value (near 0.0) = all conditions produce the same result. Also add compute_diagnostic_value_report(all_results_by_condition) -> dict that computes this for all cases.

4. Create virtual_genesis/eval/runners/run_self_benchmark_cycle.py with run_self_benchmark_cycle(task_cases, conditions=None) -> dict. Ties it together: (a) Runs current eval via compare_conditions, (b) Analyzes results for anomaly candidates via generate_anomaly_candidate_report, (c) Discovers blind spots via discover_blind_spots, (d) Generates new benchmark cases from anomalies using benchmark_generator, (e) Runs conditions against new cases, (f) Computes diagnostic value for all cases, (g) Returns combined report. Gate behind use_self_benchmarking parameter.

5. Create tests/test_self_benchmarking.py with 20+ tests covering: benchmark_generator produces valid TaskCase objects from anomaly candidates, handles empty anomalies, handles various source_types; blind_spot_discovery finds untested combos and suspiciously easy regions; diagnostic_value computes correctly for uniform results (value=0) and varied results (value>0); run_self_benchmark_cycle integrates all components end-to-end.
6. Update virtual_genesis/eval/reports/__init__.py and virtual_genesis/eval/runners/__init__.py (if they have imports) to include new modules. Ensure all existing 163 tests still pass.
acceptance_criteria: generate_from_anomaly_candidates returns valid TaskCase objects with diagnostic_purpose=['anomaly_derived', 'self_benchmark']
discover_blind_spots flags 100% success regions as suspiciously easy
compute_diagnostic_value returns 0.0 for uniform results and >0 for varied results
run_self_benchmark_cycle completes an end-to-end cycle producing new benchmark cases
All 163 existing tests continue to pass
verification: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q (all tests pass including existing 163)
findings: All 202 tests pass (163 existing + 39 new). The __init__.py files for eval/reports and eval/runners only have __all__ = [] so no import updates needed. Coverage ratio edge case with empty inputs fixed to return 1.0 (vacuous truth).
